title: Detroit

jokes.text: Time to lose your fiscal cherry.
jokes.setup: Character excited about economic summit
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo applied to fiscal policy
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay combining economic and sexual terminology with good economy

jokes.text: You're gonna be like the 'fairy job-mother.'
jokes.setup: Talking about job announcements and fan reaction
jokes.punchline: Twisted fairy godmother reference for job creation
jokes.explanation: Creative mashup of fairy tale and politics with decent execution

jokes.text: I want to hear the Discovery Channel say, 'We interrupt these sharks.'
jokes.setup: Wanting maximum media coverage for job announcement
jokes.punchline: Absurd expectation that even Discovery Channel would break from shark programming
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor with perfect specificity about Discovery Channel's shark obsession

jokes.text: Thanks for sending me the speech, Mike. It's perfect... - Great. - ...ly shit.
jokes.setup: Seemingly positive feedback on speech
jokes.punchline: Completion reveals harsh criticism
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection with perfect timing and economy

jokes.text: Your first drafts are always terrible. - That's my fourth. - Oh, God.
jokes.setup: Criticism about first drafts being terrible
jokes.punchline: Revelation it's actually fourth draft, making it worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure with character-specific incompetence

jokes.text: You know what? Next time I'll get a clown and cheer you up. Oh, good, a two-clown entourage.
jokes.setup: Sarcastic suggestion to get a clown
jokes.punchline: Implication that there's already one clown (the trainer)
jokes.explanation: Sharp insult comedy with good structure and character voice

jokes.text: So you're the guy who keeps burning my tongue.
jokes.setup: Meeting the man who makes coffee cup lids
jokes.punchline: Personal blame for universal hot coffee experience
jokes.explanation: Relatable observational humor with Selina's self-centered perspective

jokes.text: - You see that tie? - Yeah. - It's hideous. - Horrendous. - Suits him. - Yeah, it does.
jokes.setup: Team discussing Andrew's terrible tie
jokes.punchline: 'Suits him' works literally and figuratively
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay with team bonding over shared disdain

jokes.text: Andrew Meyer, dick on fire.
jokes.setup: Describing Andrew after seeing his terrible tie
jokes.punchline: Bizarre, aggressive nickname
jokes.explanation: Memorable absurdist insult with great economy

jokes.text: The metal skeleton, you know, within the structure itself is useless, but then you put this black fabric hexagonal-shaped thing on it, and what do you have? An umbrella. And it's a winning team. And I like to think of it as a metaphor.
jokes.setup: Character trying to sound profound about business
jokes.punchline: Overwrought explanation of basic umbrella construction as metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing character's pretentious attempt at wisdom

jokes.text: She was a vicious bitch and a fuckin' drunk. - Yep. But that's sad news.
jokes.setup: News of journalist Emily Lafuente's death
jokes.punchline: Brutal honest assessment followed by perfunctory sympathy
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy showing character's callousness masked by token respect

jokes.text: You don't have to do the whispering. It's just me.
jokes.setup: Someone whispering about the dead journalist
jokes.punchline: Permission to speak normally because no one important is listening
jokes.explanation: Sharp character moment revealing self-awareness and status

jokes.text: Where do you get your news, from a guy on a horse?
jokes.setup: Someone is late hearing about the shooting
jokes.punchline: Absurd suggestion of pre-modern news delivery
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist insult with perfect specificity and timing

jokes.text: How about I have the veep put her arms like in 'Platoon' and scream, 'Why?' Too wordy?
jokes.setup: Asked to write statement about shooting, told not to be wordy
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously over-the-top dramatic gesture suggested as non-wordy alternative
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation with perfect pop culture reference and self-aware humor about wordiness

jokes.text: Catherine, I can't send Mike and Ben to Coachella. But you, you know, you're hip. You're 'deck,' you know? Deck's a thing, right?
jokes.setup: Selina trying to explain why Catherine gets music assignments
jokes.punchline: Attempt to use slang that doesn't exist, then asking for confirmation
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing Selina's desperation to seem cool and completely missing the mark

jokes.text: Okay, remember her husband fondled your left breast? What do you think, I'm gonna forget that? I've got a thumbprint there.
jokes.setup: Gary reminding Selina about Finnish woman
jokes.punchline: Absurd claim about permanent physical evidence of groping
jokes.explanation: Strong escalation humor with Gary's dramatic over-detail about the incident

jokes.text: I am giving a talk on economics. 'The Finnish Wilf.' - Er, what...? - It's 'wolf.' Oh, hello. - 'The Finnish Wolf.' - Yes. - And there is my book. - Oh. - And what is that called? - 'The Finnish Wilf.' - It's 'wolf.' - Yeah, I got it.
jokes.setup: Minna explaining her book title
jokes.punchline: Repeated mispronunciation despite corrections
jokes.explanation: Solid running gag showing communication failure and stubbornness

jokes.text: I want a lanyard with a retractable one. Do you have any idea who I am?!
jokes.setup: Jonah frustrated about badge situation
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration of importance over trivial badge preference
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Jonah's inflated self-importance over petty details

jokes.text: Jonah, come here. You know, sometimes I feel like there's a nine-year-old boy inside of you just operating the levers.
jokes.setup: Jonah's childish behavior over the badge
jokes.punchline: Metaphor of child controlling adult body like machine
jokes.explanation: Excellent character insult with vivid, original metaphor that perfectly captures Jonah's immaturity

jokes.text: Minimum six-second handshake, and I want a toothy smile. Perfect. Because we've got to prove they both don't hate each other. - Absolutely. - Okay. And this is it. - This is it. - That's it. - This is it. - This is it.
jokes.setup: Planning photo op between rivals
jokes.punchline: Repetitive confirmation that spirals into meaninglessness
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation showing how political planning becomes absurd repetition

jokes.text: And if there was, by the way, it would be a 'Meyer-Maddox' feud. Yeah, yeah. She would go first. That's right.
jokes.setup: Discussing potential feud naming
jokes.punchline: Insistence on proper alphabetical/hierarchical order even for feuds
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing petty concern with status even in hypothetical conflicts

jokes.text: I look tense, because... yes, well, one, I have broad shoulders, and, two, because that's the job. The job is what makes me look tense. But underneath all the work tension, I'm really very relaxed.
jokes.setup: Amy defending her tense appearance
jokes.punchline: Increasingly defensive explanation that contradicts itself
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Amy's defensive rationalization

jokes.text: To give guns to babies? Well, only in Arizona.
jokes.setup: Minna's exaggerated question about American gun culture
jokes.punchline: Selina's deadpan regional stereotype confirmation
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect timing and regional specificity

jokes.text: - Minna, I've made a joke. - And me also, I made a joke... for giving the guns to the babies. Because I know you don't give the guns to the babies.
jokes.setup: Both women trying to acknowledge their joke-making
jokes.punchline: Over-explanation of obvious joke ruins the humor
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing how explaining jokes kills them

jokes.text: He's not just a personal trainer, he's like a personal Jesus.
jokes.setup: Selina praising her trainer Ray
jokes.punchline: Religiously absurd comparison for fitness instructor
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist comparison showing Selina's hyperbolic praise

jokes.text: Maybe you should work on North Korea first... get them to relax, and work your way up to Amy.
jokes.setup: Ray offering to help Amy with tension
jokes.punchline: Suggestion that Amy is harder case than hostile nation
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke with perfect character specificity and geopolitical comparison

jokes.text: Just so sad. I hate murder.
jokes.setup: Discussing journalist's death
jokes.punchline: Stating obvious universal position as personal stance
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of absurdly obvious statement

jokes.text: Shit the fuck... she said that?
jokes.setup: Learning Selina made controversial gun comment
jokes.punchline: Immediate panic and profanity eruption
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing immediate political damage assessment

jokes.text: I haven't bent this far since I was five centimeters dilated.
jokes.setup: Selina doing exercises with trainer
jokes.punchline: TMI comparison to childbirth measurement
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe humor with perfect TMI specificity from Selina

jokes.text: How about we turn out the lights and see?
jokes.setup: Selina saying she feels like she's glowing from exercise
jokes.punchline: Ray's flirtatious suggestion to test the glow
jokes.explanation: Solid setup for the romantic tension with modest humor

jokes.text: Marimba... that alarm that's on your phone when you wake up. It's like, 'Wake up, America!'
jokes.setup: Asked to explain 'Marimba America'
jokes.punchline: Pathetic attempt to justify nonsensical phrase with weak wordplay
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Mike's desperation to justify bad writing

jokes.text: I wrote a soundbite once and had the entire crowd in tears. Even the police horses.
jokes.setup: Boasting about speechwriting ability
jokes.punchline: Absurd claim about affecting non-human audience
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect absurd detail about police horses

jokes.text: I can improve your posture 6,000%.
jokes.setup: Ray boasting about his training abilities
jokes.punchline: Mathematically impossible improvement percentage
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist exaggeration showing Ray's over-confidence

jokes.text: Well, so can I. I can just take off my shoes.
jokes.setup: Ray claiming he can improve posture dramatically
jokes.punchline: Selina's practical solution acknowledging her height insecurity
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment with perfect economy and self-awareness

jokes.text: I spend more time with you than any of my other friends. That technically makes you my best friend.
jokes.setup: Selina talking to Amy about friendship
jokes.punchline: Depressing logical conclusion about their relationship
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Selina's sad social reality

jokes.text: - We're fucking. - Uh-huh...
jokes.setup: Selina casually mentioning she's sleeping with Ray
jokes.punchline: Amy's completely unsurprised reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat showing Amy's weary acceptance of Selina's predictability

jokes.text: 'Cause he'll be, 'Oh, a crisis. I need a third iPad.'
jokes.setup: Selina explaining why Dan can't know about Ray
jokes.punchline: Perfect impression of Dan's crisis over-reaction and materialism
jokes.explanation: Excellent character impression with perfect specificity about Dan's crisis behavior

jokes.text: I wrote it. - Oh. Well, it's got some good bits.
jokes.setup: Someone criticizing Mike's writing, then learning he wrote it
jokes.punchline: Awkward backpedaling with faint praise
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment with realistic social fumbling

jokes.text: Or how about a drone strike?
jokes.setup: Suggesting restraining order against Jonah
jokes.punchline: Escalation to military action against annoying reporter
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect dark humor about government overreach

jokes.text: You're a sex trafficker now. How low can you go? Well, however it is, Amy, I'm still higher than you.
jokes.setup: Amy accusing Dan of procuring Ray for Selina
jokes.punchline: Dan admitting moral flexibility while maintaining superiority
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Dan's competitive nature even in moral bankruptcy

jokes.text: We know. You're Ray-curious.
jokes.setup: Gary defending Ray enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Play on 'bi-curious' with Ray's name
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with perfect economy and character targeting

jokes.text: Sorry, I didn't mean to strike a nerve there. You didn't strike a nerve. I'm just saying... because you're in shape doesn't mean you don't know that 'IMF' means 'International Money Fund.' 'Monetary.' Close.
jokes.setup: Someone condescending to Ray about economics
jokes.punchline: Ray's defensive response reveals he actually doesn't know the acronym
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Ray's insecurity and actual ignorance

jokes.text: Stay small, Selina! - Oh, my God! - Go, go, go! - Ow, ow, ow! My hair! My hair! My hair!
jokes.setup: Protesters attacking, security trying to protect VP
jokes.punchline: In crisis, Selina's main concern is her hair
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Selina's vanity even in danger

jokes.text: Give me your fuckin' gun! I'll shoot his balls off!
jokes.setup: Staff member angry about attack on VP
jokes.punchline: Immediate escalation to extreme violence and emasculation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing staff loyalty through extreme violence fantasy

jokes.text: Catherine just did what any good daughter would do. - What...? - You punched that motherfucker. - You punched that guy square in the face. - No!
jokes.setup: Praising Catherine for defending her mother
jokes.punchline: Catherine's horrified denial reveals she's not violent
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Catherine's pacifist nature vs. others' assumptions

jokes.text: Honey, that means you're really good at it and you didn't even know it. No!
jokes.setup: Catherine denying she punched anyone
jokes.punchline: Suggestion that unconscious violence is even more impressive
jokes.explanation: Good escalation comedy with Catherine's continued horror

jokes.text: Now I feel like you're attacking me. It's kind of irritating. Okay, just back off.
jokes.setup: Gary apologizing for not protecting her better
jokes.punchline: Selina treating apology as additional attack
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Selina's self-centered interpretation of concern

jokes.text: Can you not hear me singing in the wires?
jokes.setup: Kent's strange phone behavior
jokes.punchline: Poetic/mystical description of telecommunications
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist dialogue perfectly fitting Kent's analytical yet poetic character

jokes.text: You just picked a fight with the jumpiest hundred million people I can possibly think of.
jokes.setup: Selina's gun comments creating political crisis
jokes.punchline: Perfect description of gun owners' sensitivity
jokes.explanation: Excellent political observation with perfect specificity and characterization

jokes.text: I wish we were still on abortion. That was easy.
jokes.setup: Dealing with gun controversy
jokes.punchline: Nostalgia for 'easier' controversial topic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political irony about relative difficulty of controversial topics

jokes.text: I need 'Ray-creation.' Just had it.
jokes.setup: Selina saying she needs rest and relaxation
jokes.punchline: Pun combining recreation with Ray's name/sexual services
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with character-specific sexual reference

jokes.text: In years to come, a therapist will have me acting this out with dolls.
jokes.setup: Catherine processing being attacked by protester
jokes.punchline: Specific prediction of future therapy techniques
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with specific therapeutic detail from Catherine's perspective

jokes.text: I throw one punch, and all of a sudden I'm 'America's Next Top Redneck.'
jokes.setup: Catherine complaining about new violent image
jokes.punchline: Perfect reality show parody title for her situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent pop culture parody with perfect specificity to Catherine's unwanted violent reputation

jokes.text: That is not your shoe.
jokes.setup: Catherine noticing something wrong in room
jokes.punchline: Evidence of Ray's hasty departure from sex
jokes.explanation: Good visual evidence gag with perfect economy for awkward discovery

jokes.text: What, the Pilates made your shirt fly off?
jokes.setup: Finding Ray shirtless with Selina
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic explanation for obvious post-sex state
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic line with perfect delivery for awkward situation

jokes.text: Mom, not the help. Jesus, that's tacky.
jokes.setup: Catherine seeing mother with trainer
jokes.punchline: Class-conscious criticism of affair
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Catherine's snobbery and moral standards

jokes.text: Nobody likes sex, let's disperse.
jokes.setup: Awkward situation with everyone uncomfortable
jokes.punchline: Absurd universal declaration to end awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist line with perfect deadpan delivery to diffuse tension

jokes.text: Kenny G can blow the storm up.
jokes.setup: Suggesting jazz trio as distraction
jokes.punchline: Confusing Kenny G with weather control
jokes.explanation: Absurd misunderstanding with mild humor value

jokes.text: It's like a man, but it's very tall. - Rudolph. - No, no.
jokes.setup: Minna describing Jonah using European folklore
jokes.punchline: Confused American trying to guess with wrong Christmas character
jokes.explanation: Good cultural miscommunication humor with Christmas confusion

jokes.text: In your country, people fuck snow.
jokes.setup: Minna criticizing American honesty in politics
jokes.punchline: Selina's aggressive counter-stereotype about Finland
jokes.explanation: Excellent aggressive comeback with perfect absurdist stereotype and Selina's diplomatic brutality

jokes.text: I want for your comfort to make you aware that today I purchased ear pligs. - Ear plugs. - Oh, ear plugs. Okay.
jokes.setup: Minna trying to be considerate about noise
jokes.punchline: Mispronunciation reveals what she expects to hear
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with cultural awkwardness and mispronunciation

jokes.text: Dan? I am not gonna talk to radio stations that have eagles in the logo or call themselves the 'voice of reason.'
jokes.setup: Catherine rejecting certain media appearances
jokes.punchline: Specific criteria for right-wing media identification
jokes.explanation: Excellent political satire with perfect specificity about conservative media branding

jokes.text: Uh, yes, you are going to a 'fucking gun show,' even if I have to put a gun to your fucking head.
jokes.setup: Selina refusing to go to gun show
jokes.punchline: Threatening gun violence to force gun show attendance
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic escalation with perfect logical contradiction

jokes.text: You say that once a week.
jokes.setup: Someone declaring this is when Selina's campaign begins
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the repeated false starts
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about political campaign rhetoric patterns

jokes.text: How the fuck do you screw up a handshake, Mike? It's four fingers and a thumb.
jokes.setup: Failed photo op with handshake
jokes.punchline: Breaking down handshake to most basic anatomical level
jokes.explanation: Excellent breakdown of simple task with perfect anatomical specificity

jokes.text: And if you don't like my tone, you're really not going to like Guantanamo Bay.
jokes.setup: Staff member being aggressive about gun show
jokes.punchline: Threatening torture facility for staff disagreement
jokes.explanation: Good dark escalation with government overreach humor

jokes.text: Don't say, 'Freeze, or I'll protect your fucking head off.'
jokes.setup: Instructions to use 'protection' instead of 'gun'
jokes.punchline: Absurd example of euphemism gone wrong
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist example showing how euphemism creates nonsense

jokes.text: Look at this well-defended picnic table. Covered in all these... things that defend.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to avoid saying 'gun' at gun show
jokes.punchline: Awkward euphemisms make her sound ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy showing Selina's linguistic gymnastics

jokes.text: Although this would not have been a good present for her, 'cause she was a toddler with a temper. She was so mad. - I wasn't. - Just say you were mad. Doesn't matter.
jokes.setup: Selina making conversation about pink gun and Catherine
jokes.punchline: Forcing daughter to play along with false narrative
jokes.explanation: Good mother-daughter dynamic showing Selina's public manipulation

jokes.text: This one is upside down, so if you were doing a back bend or something...
jokes.setup: Selina examining guns at show
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous practical application for upside-down gun
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic from Selina trying to find innocent gun uses

jokes.text: Just one freaky dyke after another.
jokes.setup: Catherine observing gun show attendees
jokes.punchline: Harsh characterization revealing her judgmental side
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Catherine's snobbery but relies on slur

jokes.text: I would have told you last night, but you had Ray's cock in your ear.
jokes.setup: Andrew explaining why he didn't warn about Turville jobs
jokes.punchline: Anatomically impossible sexual metaphor for distraction
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist insult with creative anatomical impossibility

jokes.text: Talk about the GDP. Nobody knows what that means. - Do you know what that means? - Yes, I...
jokes.setup: Andrew suggesting Selina talk about GDP since it's meaningless
jokes.punchline: Being challenged reveals he probably doesn't know either
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment exposing Andrew's own ignorance

jokes.text: Oh, my God, it's the big bad 'wilf.'
jokes.setup: Seeing Minna arrive as panel moderator
jokes.punchline: Callback to her mispronounced book title with fairy tale reference
jokes.explanation: Good callback with fairy tale twist on recurring pronunciation error

jokes.text: The men and women who are behind the umbrella skeleton.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to avoid job announcement
jokes.punchline: Callback to earlier pretentious umbrella metaphor
jokes.explanation: Decent callback showing Selina grasping for earlier nonsensical content

jokes.text: Well, if we kill everybody in the room, then we might be okay.
jokes.setup: Realizing the job announcement disaster
jokes.punchline: Mass murder as damage control solution
jokes.explanation: Good dark escalation showing desperation over political damage

jokes.text: Once I got used to all the regular people and how fat they were, I really enjoyed it.
jokes.setup: Selina reflecting on gun show experience
jokes.punchline: Casual elitist observation about ordinary Americans' weight
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Selina's unconscious elitism and casual cruelty

jokes.text: You know, it's okay, it doesn't matter... oh-hh... oh, my God! That's very good.
jokes.setup: Andrew giving Selina massage
jokes.punchline: Sexual-sounding reaction to ex-husband's touch
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe moment suggesting romantic/sexual tension with ex

tmdb_overview: At an economic summit in Detroit, Selina’s staff and her family are peeved by the presence of her new personal trainer. Meanwhile, Jonah and Mike negotiate a photo-op with Selina and Maddox. Later, Catherine protects her mom from a protestor, and Selina attends a women’s gun show.
insight: Detroit's cringe machinery delivers 78 jokes across relentless character fumbles and absurdist chaos.
